{{Quote|Back to the ship... we've a new job ahead of us!|Beilert Valance|<ref name="marvel16" />}}
The '''''Kill Switch''''' was the personal [[Starship/Legends|starship]] of [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]], a feared [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] of the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Era]]. Originally owned by the [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] [[Alabar Double Ax]], the ship was appropriated and renamed after the [[Sikurdian/Legends|Sikurdian]] raider was hunted down by Valance. The former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] officer then gathered a crew and traveled across the far reaches of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in the ''Kill Switch'', filling their days with violence. By [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] Valance became obsessed with tracking down a young [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] named [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], and subsequently flew the ''Kill Switch'' into some of its most violent actions, including the utter [[Attack on Anglebay Station|destruction]] of an unarmed, neutral [[Anglebay Station|medical station]] on [[Telos IV]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Cripes! Think'a how many steaks I could buy for what that plating will cost!|Jaxxon, after his ship was attacked by the ''Kill Switch''|<ref name="marvel16" />}}
A light [[Cruiser/Legends|cruiser]],<ref name="marvel16" /> the ''Kill Switch'' was a [[Starship/Legends|starship]] used for [[Pirate/Legends|piracy]] and [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] around [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="picutorion" /> Propelled by one large [[Engine/Legends|engine]] near the rear of the craft, the ''Kill Switch'' had the kind of firepower that could swiftly disable a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]]-sized craft.<ref name="marvel16" />

==History==
{{Quote|Looks like we hit them just hard enough. I wanted them down... but intact.|Beilert Valance, after his ''Kill Switch'' shot down the ''Rabbit's Foot''|<ref name="marvel16" />}}
The ''Kill Switch'' was originally owned and operated by [[Alabar Double Ax]], a notorious [[Sikurdian/Legends|Sikurdian]] pirate who was active during the era of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. Double Ax was eventually targeted by [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] [[Beilert Valance/Legends|Beilert Valance]]—a former [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtrooper]] officer who was rebuilt as a [[Cyborg/Legends|cyborg]] after suffering grievous wounds in [[Assault on Doniphon|battle]] with the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebels]]—who pursued the pirate from [[Sikurd]] all the way to the [[Red Nebula/Legends|Red Nebula]] before apprehending them. Valance then appropriated Double Ax's ship, giving it the name ''Kill Switch''. Valance used the ship as his base of operations, putting together a crew that helped him establish himself as an elite bounty hunter by [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]].<ref name="picutorion" />

However, by the time of the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]], Valance had begun to lose his grip on reality. Tormented by his status as a cyborg—he was known for his hatred of mechanicals—Valance had become obsessed with an unnamed "[[Luke Skywalker/Legends|farmboy]] who had played a crucial part in the [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|rescue]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] from Imperial captivity.<ref name="seven" /> Valance's hatred wasn't [[Politics/Legends|political]]: rather, he despised the lad for his friendship with [[R2-D2/Legends|two]] [[C-3PO/Legends|droids]].<ref name="picutorion" /> As Valance continued his spiral into madness, he led his crew in a campaign across the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] on the ''Kill Switch'', embarking on a series of increasingly more violent missions.<ref name="seven" /> This culminated in Valance's brutal [[Attack on Anglebay Station|raid]] on the unarmed, officially neutral medical facility known as [[Anglebay Station]] on [[Telos IV]] in 0 ABY. Valance's attack on Anglebay Station slaughtered everyone within the hospital and completely destroyed the facility before he and his hunters returned to the ''Kill Switch'' and left as suddenly as they arrived.<ref name="marvel16" />

[[File:KillSwitchAttacks.jpg|thumb|left|350px|The ''Kill Switch'' chases down the ''Rabbit's Foot'' above Aduba-3.]]
Although many of Valance's crew saw the raid as senseless,<ref name="seven" /> it did uncover one valuable piece of information: one of the ''Kill Switch''{{'s}} crew, [[Remel Fud]], overheard the babblings of an [[Don-Wan Kihotay|elderly patient]] that pointed them in the direction of well-known [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], a known associate of Valance's "farmboy." An ensuing investigation led Valance to believe that his quarry—a young man named Luke Skywalker—could be found on the agrarian Outer Rim planet [[Aduba-3/Legends|Aduba-3]], and the ''Kill Switch'' immediately made haste to the [[Farm/Legends|farmworld]]. Upon arriving at Aduba-3, however, they found that some of Solo's friends were waiting for them: the ''Kill Switch'' was met above the planet by the [[Lepi/Legends|Lepi]] smuggler [[Jaxxon/Legends|Jaxxon]] and his running partner [[Amaiza Foxtrain/Legends|Amaiza Foxtrain]] in Jaxxon's ship [[Rabbit's Foot/Legends|''Rabbit's Foot'']]. Valance immediately recognized that the [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] was making a run on the ''Kill Switch'' and quickly ordered his crew to fully activate the ship's [[Deflector shield/Legends|shields]], saving the ship. The ''Kill Switch'' promptly pounded the ''Rabbit's Foot'' with its [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and disabled the ship, sending it crashing to the planet below. Valance and his crew followed, only to immediately walk into a trap: once they left the ship, Jaxxon and his compatriots stampeded a herd of [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]], which trampled most of Valance's men. Valance survived to discover that the "[[Jimm Doshun|farmboy]]" they were defending wasn't the target he was looking for, and after a long laugh, Valance returned to his ship and swiftly resumed his search across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]].<ref name="marvel16" />

Valance's pursuit eventually led him to the Outer Rim world [[Feriae Junction/Legends|Feriae Junction]]. Valance had little linking his quarry to the world except intuition: many [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlanes]] crossed at Feriae Junction, and Valance hoped that the [[Yavin blockade|ongoing Imperial blockade]] of the Rebel-held [[Yavin system/Legends|Yavin system]] could lead Skywalker to the trading post. By this point, Valance was alone, living on the ''Kill Switch'' and surviving by hunting local [[Bounty/Legends|bounties]]. Valance's hunch was proven true after several weeks, when a [[Skinker|contact]] tipped the hunter off that he had seen Skywalker on the planet. Valance swiftly tracked Skywalker to a scrap yard, only to ultimately let the Rebel leave after his [[Droid/Legends|droid]], C-3PO, stepped in to save his life.<ref name="marvel27">[[Star Wars (1977) 27|''Star Wars'' (1977) 27]]</ref>

==Commanders and crew==
The ''Kill Switch'' was originally owned an operated by the Sikurdian pirate Alabar Double Ax, before it was taken and renamed by bounty hunter Beilert Valance. A former Imperial stormtrooper officer, Valance used the ''Kill Switch'' as he made his name as one of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy's]] deadliest hunters, distinguished by his sharp instincts and virulent hatred of [[Droid/Legends|droids]].<ref name="picutorion" /> Valance populated the ''Kill Switch'' with a crew full of hardened killers, including his feared [[First Mate/Legends|first mate]] [[Slssk]], a [[Hutlarian]] former [[Gladiator/Legends|gladiator]].<ref name="past">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=swminis/article/huntwithin2|text=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 2|int=The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale: Part 2}}</ref> Also among the hunters that once called the ''Kill Switch'' home was Remel Fud, once a notorious gun-for-hire in the [[Nembus sector/Legends|Nembus sector]] who had been targeted by Valance during his days as an Imperial officer.<ref name="seven" />

==Behind the scenes==
The ''Kill Switch'' first appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 16|''Star Wars'' (1977) 16]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]] and released in [[1978]]. Although it originally went unnamed in its appearances in the [[Star Wars (1977)|Marvel ''Star Wars'']] [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] run,<ref name="marvel16" /> it was officially named in ''[[The Hunt Within: Valance's Tale]]'', a [[2004]] [[Star Wars Miniatures]] scenario written by [[Jason Fry]].<ref name="picutorion" />
